levels.label: Scattered
levels.copy: The Investor Data Room Readiness pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Basic
levels.copy: Some Investor Data Room Readiness practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Diligence-Aware
levels.copy: Core Investor Data Room Readiness practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Investor-Ready
levels.copy: Investor Data Room Readiness practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Audit-Grade
levels.copy: Investor Data Room Readiness is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.corporate-and-ownership: Reconcile every security to source documents and establish a version-controlled corporate index.
recommendations.financial-and-tax: Reconcile historical accounts, bank data, and the driver-based model; disclose material contingencies.
recommendations.commercial-and-market: Link contracts, cohorts, concentration, and pipeline claims to source systems and named owners.
recommendations.ip-technology-and-risk: Close IP assignment gaps and prepare transparent security, privacy, and regulatory risk packs.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
